title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men charged with kidnapping and assault after allegedly attacking a man near Winter Park.
Victim, 28, was reportedly beaten and restrained with an extension cord, sustaining non-life-threatening injuries.
Suspects include Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett; arraignment set for Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested at Logan Airport by ICE for immigration-related issues.
Rosales was detained due to a pending DACA renewal and a 2017 removal order she was unaware of.
Ardila was detained for allegedly overstaying his visa while awaiting an asylum application decision.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Interstellar" (2014) and "Lawrence of Arabia" (1962) are recommended post-'The Odyssey' films for their thematic parallels.
"O Brother, Where Art Thou?" (2000) humorously reimagines Homer's tale with George Clooney as Ulysses Everett McGill.
"The Searchers" (1956) and "The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie" (2004) are cited for their journey motifs akin to Homer's epic.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 11 games with a doubleheader sweep against the Tampa Bay Rays.
Jarren Duran and Carlos Narvaez significantly contributed to the 10-0 win in Game 1, aided by Jake Bennett's pitching.
Wilyer Abreu hit two home runs in Game 2, leading to a 5-3 win and solidifying the team's wild-card position.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early are recovering well, with Suarez possibly returning on July 21.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo show progress in rehab; Houck is throwing from the mound twice weekly.
Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, Isaiah Kiner-Falefa, and Marcelo Mayer remain out with no return timetable.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit three times, including a home run, helping Red Sox win over Rays in Game 1.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 across both games, adding an RBI and two runs for the Red Sox.
Pitcher Jake Bennett threw 6 scoreless innings with one hit, aiding Boston's bullpen strategy.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Kraft supports Mike Vrabel despite offseason controversy involving NFL reporter Dianna Russini.
Vrabel denied affair allegations with Russini, leading to his absence on Day 3 of the NFL draft.
Patriots training camp starts next weekend as Vrabel remains head coach after reaching Super Bowl last season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Sam Neill's 'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople' is now streaming on Prime Video and Plex.
Curry Barker's horror hit 'Obsession' is streaming on Peacock, featuring Inde Navarrette's breakout role.
Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky,' a crime series now available on Apple TV.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin, 25, from Concord, violated New Hampshire's Civil Rights Act in a 2024 assault on a transgender woman.
Lufkin was ordered to avoid contact with the victim and fined, with $4,000 of a $5,000 fine suspended for compliance.
Lufkin also faced criminal charges, receiving a 12-month sentence with part of it suspended upon pleading guilty to assault charges.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox won their 10th consecutive game against the Tampa Bay Rays, nearing a .500 record for the season.
Boston's trade deadline plans are 'fluid,' with potential moves before the August 3 deadline still undecided.
ESPN reports Boston may look for a right-handed hitter, but a significant trade involving Aroldis Chapman is unlikely.
